text:
MEDICAL ONCOLOGY — OFFICE VISIT NOTE
Metastatic Melanoma, Stage IV (M1c) | BRAF V600E+

**ATTENDING: [Oncologist]**
**PATIENT: Renata Okonkwo | DOB: [on file] | MRN: [on file]**

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT / REASON FOR VISIT:
Routine oncology follow-up. Review of recent PET-CT results. Ongoing discussion of nivolumab maintenance duration and treatment discontinuation planning.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:

Ms. Okonkwo is a 56-year-old woman with metastatic melanoma (Stage IV M1c, BRAF V600E-mutant, CDKN2A/PTEN/NF1 co-mutated, TMB-high at 28 mut/Mb) who presents today for a landmark visit. She has been on nivolumab maintenance (Opdivo 480 mg IV q4 weeks) for approximately 23 months following 4 cycles of ipilimumab/nivolumab (ipi/nivo, CheckMate-067 dosing: ipi 3 mg/kg + nivo 1 mg/kg q3 weeks), which itself was initiated as second-line therapy after progression on dabrafenib/trametinib (Tafinlar/Mekinist) at approximately 8 months.

Today's visit is centered around the results of her most recent PET-CT (whole body FDG with concurrent MRI brain), which was obtained approximately 3 months after her last comprehensive restaging. That prior CT at roughly the 24-month mark had shown a near-complete response — a solitary residual 0.3 cm hepatic hypodensity in the right lobe was the only finding of note, no FDG-avid disease at that time having not yet been assessed metabolically.

PET-CT results reviewed in detail today:
- No FDG-avid lesions anywhere in the body
- The previously noted 0.3 cm hepatic hypodensity shows SUVmax below background liver activity — effectively metabolically silent; consistent with fibrotic scar
- No avid axillary, cervical, inguinal, or peritoneal disease
- MRI brain: no intracranial metastases, unchanged from all prior scans
- LDH: 131 U/L (normal, stable, down from 389 U/L at progression on BRAF/MEK therapy nearly 2 years ago)

In summary: complete metabolic response (CMR) by PET criteria, representing 27 months total on immunotherapy with no evidence of viable tumor.

Ms. Okonkwo's disease history in brief: she was initially diagnosed with deeply invasive nodular melanoma of the mid-back (Breslow 5.8 mm, ulcerated, Clark V, mitotic rate 8/mm2, LVI present), with Stage IV M1c disease at presentation encompassing bilateral axillary and inguinal lymphadenopathy, multiple subcutaneous/cutaneous trunk nodules, and hepatic metastases (up to 2.4 cm, 6 lesions). First-line dabrafenib/trametinib was chosen given BRAF V600E positivity, high disease burden, and desire for rapid disease control. She had an impressive early partial response but progressed at 8 months, with acquired resistance attributed to a MEK1 C121S resistance mutation and BRAF amplification on liquid biopsy (Guardant360). She transitioned to ipi/nivo and achieved an objective response, though this was complicated by Grade 3 immune-mediated colitis during cycle 3 (requiring hospitalization, IV methylprednisolone, and ultimately infliximab 5 mg/kg for steroid-refractory disease, with resolution over 7 days inpatient). Ipilimumab was permanently discontinued. She resumed nivolumab monotherapy (480 mg q4 weeks) after steroids tapered below 10 mg prednisone. She has maintained a deepening partial response over the ensuing 23 months on maintenance nivo, progressing to a near-complete response and now achieving a complete metabolic response.

She reports no new symptoms today. She feels well, has returned to full-time work as a landscape architect, and is physically active. No diarrhea or abdominal symptoms (colitis in full remission per gastroenterology clearance ~6 months ago). No fatigue beyond normal. No rash. No joint symptoms.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
- Constitutional: No fevers, chills, or night sweats. Weight stable.
- Dermatologic: No new skin lesions, rashes, or pigmented lesions of concern. Last dermatology exam 6 weeks ago — clear.
- GI: Bowel habits at personal baseline (1-2 stools/day), formed. No blood in stool, no cramping, no urgency. No nausea or vomiting.
- Endocrine: Mild fatigue and cold intolerance improved over recent months. No palpitations.
- Musculoskeletal: No new joint pain or stiffness to suggest inflammatory arthritis.
- Neurologic: No headache, visual changes, focal weakness, or cognitive changes.
- Pulmonary: No cough, dyspnea, or pleuritic chest pain.
- Genitourinary: No hematuria or proteinuria (urinalysis at last lab check: normal).
- Ophthalmologic: No new visual symptoms. History of anterior uveitis — no recurrence, most recently cleared by ophthalmology.

---

PAST ONCOLOGIC HISTORY (summarized):
1. Stage IV M1c melanoma (BRAF V600E+), diagnosed age 55
   - Line 1: Dabrafenib/trametinib × ~8 months → progression with acquired resistance
   - Line 2: Ipi 3 mg/kg + nivo 1 mg/kg q3w × 3 cycles (cycle 4 held due to Grade 3 colitis) → ipi permanently discontinued
   - Maintenance: Nivolumab 480 mg q4w (23 months and counting)
   - Notable irAEs: Grade 2 dermatitis (cycle 2, topical steroids only), Grade 3 immune-mediated colitis (cycle 3, hospitalized, infliximab required), Grade 2 hypothyroidism (on nivo maintenance, managed with levothyroxine dose increase)
   - Notable targeted therapy toxicity: Grade 2 pyrexia (dabrafenib-induced fever), cutaneous SCCs × 2 (left forearm, right shin; excised with clear margins)

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
- General: Well-appearing woman, no acute distress. Alert and conversational. Normal habitus, healthy appearance.
- Vitals: BP 118/74 mmHg, HR 68 bpm, T 36.8°C, RR 14, SpO2 99% on room air. Weight stable from prior visit.
- HEENT: No cervical or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy palpable. Prior right level II node (2.2 cm at its worst) is not palpable. Oropharynx clear. No oral ulcers.
- Skin: Multiple healing and healed scars consistent with prior excisions (primary melanoma mid-back, SCC sites on left forearm and right shin). No new pigmented lesions of concern on general inspection. No rash.
- Lymph nodes: No axillary, inguinal, or epitrochlear lymphadenopathy. Right axillary region soft without palpable nodes (previously largest 4.2 cm).
- Chest/Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheeze or crackles.
- Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatomegaly or splenomegaly. No palpable masses. Normal bowel sounds. Prior right upper quadrant discomfort (from hepatic metastases during progression) entirely resolved.
- Extremities: No peripheral edema. No joint effusions or synovitis.
- Neurologic: Cranial nerves II-XII intact. Normal gait and coordination.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS (current visit / recent draws):
- CBC: WBC 6.2 × 10⁹/L, Hgb 13.4 g/dL, plt 241 × 10⁹/L — all within normal limits
- CMP: Na 139, K 4.1, Cl 103, CO2 25, BUN 14, creatinine 0.87 mg/dL (eGFR >60), glucose 94
- Liver function: AST 26 U/L, ALT 29 U/L, total bilirubin 0.7 mg/dL, alk phos 72 U/L — all normal (notable improvement from AST/ALT 68/72 at the time of ipi/nivo initiation, consistent with resolved hepatic disease burden)
- LDH: 131 U/L (normal; down from 389 U/L at nadir of BRAF/MEK failure)
- TSH: 2.4 mIU/L (therapeutic on levothyroxine 112 mcg daily; recall she had pre-existing hypothyroidism and developed Grade 2 checkpoint-induced hypothyroidism during nivo maintenance, currently well-managed)
- Free T4: 1.1 ng/dL (normal)
- Urinalysis: No proteinuria, no hematuria — no evidence of immune nephritis
- Cortisol (AM): Not rechecked today, was normal at last check

---

IMAGING / DISEASE STATUS:
PET-CT (whole body FDG) with concurrent gadolinium-enhanced MRI brain: Complete metabolic response. No FDG-avid lesions. Residual 0.3 cm hepatic hypodensity — metabolically silent, SUVmax below background, consistent with scar/fibrosis. No brain metastases. No evidence of active disease anywhere. This represents the deepest response achieved in this patient's course.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

1. METASTATIC MELANOMA, Stage IV M1c (BRAF V600E+) — COMPLETE METABOLIC RESPONSE

Ms. Okonkwo has achieved what is a remarkable outcome: a complete metabolic response following a protracted course of immunotherapy in the setting of M1c melanoma. Her clinical trajectory — initial BRAF/MEK therapy with rapid partial response, acquired resistance at 8 months, transition to ipi/nivo combination with Grade 3 colitis and three-quarter course completion, followed by 23 months of nivolumab maintenance with progressively deepening response — is illustrative of the complex management decisions that characterize this disease.

Treatment discontinuation discussion:
We had a detailed and thorough discussion today regarding discontinuation of nivolumab. We reviewed the available evidence from long-term follow-up of CheckMate-067 and other pivotal trials, acknowledging that prospective data on elective discontinuation after achieving complete response remain limited. Key points discussed:
- She has now received ~24 months of PD-1 monotherapy (23 months nivo maintenance + prior combination exposure)
- PET-CT confirms CMR — metabolically no evidence of disease
- Estimated relapse risk after complete response and discontinuation is in the range of 25-35% based on available retrospective and registry data, with the recognition that many relapses are re-treatable with PD-1 blockade
- Ongoing treatment carries continued, albeit lower than combination, risk of immune-related toxicity — her history of Grade 3 colitis and hypothyroidism are taken into account
- Quality of life and her personal preference are central to this decision

After thorough discussion, with input from a second opinion obtained at an NCI-designated melanoma center of excellence approximately 3 months ago, the patient has decided to DISCONTINUE nivolumab today. She received her last infusion of Opdivo 480 mg IV this visit. Total duration of PD-1 blockade: approximately 24 months (nivo maintenance); total immunotherapy exposure approximately 27 months.

This decision is supported by: CMR on PET, normal LDH, absence of any active irAEs, excellent functional status (ECOG 0), and patient preference after fully informed discussion.

2. SURVEILLANCE PLAN POST-DISCONTINUATION:
- CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast every 3 months for the first 2 years off therapy
- MRI brain with gadolinium every 6 months for 2 years (given prior M1c disease and ongoing risk)
- LDH and comprehensive labs (CBC, CMP, LFTs, TSH) quarterly
- Dermatology every 4 months — ongoing risk of primary melanoma/new primary given history of multiple dysplastic nevi and prior BRAF-inhibitor-related SCCs
- Patient instructed to report immediately: new lumps, bumps, skin changes, neurological symptoms, GI symptoms (any return of diarrhea), shortness of breath, or visual changes

3. HYPOTHYROIDISM:
Well-controlled on levothyroxine 112 mcg daily. TSH 2.4 mIU/L today. Continue current dose. Endocrinology follow-up every 6 months. Levothyroxine continued regardless of nivolumab discontinuation, as checkpoint-induced hypothyroidism is generally permanent.

4. COLITIS IN REMISSION:
Gastroenterology has cleared Ms. Okonkwo from active colitis follow-up. She is no longer on any immunosuppressive therapy. If diarrhea recurs — even in the absence of further immunotherapy — GI re-evaluation warranted to distinguish late flare from other etiologies. She is aware.

5. PSYCHOSOCIAL / PATIENT EDUCATION:
Ms. Okonkwo expressed both gratitude and a degree of anxiety regarding stopping treatment that had been working. This is entirely expected and was validated. We reviewed the robust data supporting that the immunological memory established by checkpoint blockade can persist after drug discontinuation. She was referred to an oncology social worker for support during this transition. She has a robust support network and remains fully employed and active.

Return to clinic in 3 months (with repeat CT imaging at that time). Patient has my direct contact information and that of the on-call team for urgent concerns.

---

**Electronically signed: [Attending Oncologist, MD]**
**Department of Medical Oncology**
**[Institution]**

Time spent: 60 minutes (>50% counseling and coordination of care)
